Clinician's Notes: Lumbar spine curvature is decreased. Some osteophytes of the vertebral body are degenerated. No spondylolisthesis is seen. No vertebral body compression fracture is seen. No malignant lesion of the vertebrae is seen. No infectious lesion of the vertebral body or intervertebral disc is seen. The posterior wall and pedicles are preserved. The lumbar intervertebral discs have decreased height and decreased signal intensity on T2W due to degeneration. Mild disc bulging at L4/5 and L5/S1 causing spinal canal stenosis, currently no nerve root compression is seen. No mass lesion is seen in the spinal canal. Conus medullaris, nerve roots, and cauda equina: no abnormal mass is seen. No fluid collection or abnormal mass is seen in the paravertebral muscles. Both sacroiliac joints have normal morphology and signal intensity.

The physiological curve of the lumbar spine is within normal limits. Degenerative osteophytes on the vertebral body edges of the lumbar spine. The lumbar vertebral bodies have signals within normal limits. No vertebral compression or spondylolisthesis is observed. The L4/L5 intervertebral disc is degenerated, with a central herniation causing spinal canal stenosis, with the narrowest diameter of the spinal canal being approximately 7 mm, compressing the L4 and L5 nerve roots bilaterally. There is an annular tear of the L4/L5 intervertebral disc. The remaining lumbar intervertebral discs have normal height and signal. No abnormality is seen in the soft tissues surrounding the vertebral bodies. The sacroiliac joints are unremarkable bilaterally.
